sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.24 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: The spectrum\/ of a finite group is the set of orders of its elements. Does there exist a finite group$G$whose spectrum coincides with the spectrum of a finite simple exceptional group$L$of Lie type, but$G$is not isomorphic to$L$? { A.\,V.\,Vasil'ev} Yes, for example, for$L={}^3D_4(2)$(V.\,D.\,Mazurov, %%Algebra Logika, { 52}, no.\,5 (2013), 601--605; English transl., Algebra Logic, { 52}, no.\,5 ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
